translations.translation:
Zhou Xuan (Konghe)
周宣 (孔和)
Lived AD ?–ca.240
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
byZL181
onOctober 5th, 2025

Zhou Xuan, styledKonghe, was fromLe’an[樂安].
He served as an official for his commandery. TheGrand Administrator,Yang Pei, dreamt that a person said, “On the first day of the eighth month,Excellency Caowill arrive. He will surely give you, sir, a walking cane and medicinal wine.” He sent for[Zhou] Xuanto divine this. At the time, the treacherous Yellow Scarves rose up.[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “Walking canes help the weak stand up and medicine cures people of sicknesses. On the first day of the eighth month, the traitors will certainly be vanquished.” On that date, the traitors were indeed defeated.
Later,Liu ZhenofDongping[東平] dreamt of a snake growing four legs and burrowing itself inside a gate. He sent for[Zhou] Xuanto divine this.[Zhou] Xuansaid, “This dream is for the nation, not a matter of your household, sir! You should kill women who act as traitors!” Soon after, the female traitors,Zheng and Jiang, were both suppressed. This is because a snake is an omen of women and legs are not supposed to be on snakes.
TheCivil Emperorasked[Zhou] Xuan, “I dreamt that two palace roof tiles fell to the ground and transformed into a pair of mandarin ducks. What does it mean?”[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “In the rear palace, there will be violent death.” TheEmperorsaid, “I deluded you, sir!” But[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “Dreams have meanings. If they are given form through words, then they bode fortune or misfortune.” He had not finished talking when theDirector of the Yellow Gatesreported that palace ladies killed each other.
Not long after, theEmperorasked again, “I dreamt of a green aura conjoining itself to the heavens from the ground last night.”[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “In the realm under the heavens, there will be a noble woman who will die wronged.” At the time, theEmperorhad sent a messenger with an imperial document to bestow it1toEmpressZhen. When he heard[Zhou] Xuantalk, he regretted this and sent people after the messenger. But they did notreach.•••
The character for “bestow” [賜] is also used in the phrase “bestow death” [賜死], which is forcing somebody to commit suicide.↩
TheEmperorasked again, “I dreamt that I was rubbing away the inscription of a cash coin. I wanted to remove it, but it only got brighter. What does it mean?”[Zhou] Xuanlooked despondent and did not reply. When theEmperorasked once more,[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “This is a family matter ofYour Majesty. Even though you want your intentions fulfilled, theEmpress Dowagerwill not heed you. This is why the inscription that you wanted to remove instead became brighter.” At the time, theEmperorwanted to punish his younger brother,[Cao] Zhi, for crimes, but he was compelled by theEmpress Dowagerto only give a reduction in peerage rank.
[Zhou] Xuanserved asHousehold Gentlemanand was made subordinate to theGrand Scribe.
Someone once asked[Zhou] Xuan, “Last night, I saw a straw dog in my dream. How would you divine it?”[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “Sir, you will want to have fabulous food!” Soon after, when he went out to travel, he indeed came across sumptuous meals. Later, he asked[Zhou] Xuanagain, “Last night, I saw a straw dog in my dream again. Why?”[Zhou] Xuansaid, “Sir, you will want to fall off a vehicle and snap your foot. You should be very careful against that.” Soon after, it indeed went according to what[Zhou] Xuansaid. Later, he asked[Zhou] Xuanagain, “Last night, I saw a straw dog in my dream again. Why?”[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “Sir, your house will catch on fire. You should strive well to defend against it!” Soon after, a fire erupted. He told[Zhou] Xuansaid, “In those three instances, I did not dream. I was rather testing you a bit, sir, and nothing more. So how were you proven right for them?”[Zhou] Xuanreplied, “The gods and spirits caused you, sir, to speak thusly. This is why they were no different than real dreams.” He asked[Zhou] Xuanagain, “I dreamt of straw dogs thrice, yet your divinations were not consistent. Why?”[Zhou] Xuansaid, “A straw dogs is an object to be sacrificed to the gods. Therefore, sir, for your first dream, you would receive much food. When the sacrificial offerings are finished, then the the straw dog is run over by a vehicle. Therefore, for your next dream, you would fall off a vehicle and snap your foot. After the straw dog is run over by a vehicle, it will surely be carried away to become fuel for fire. Therefore, for your last dream, I feared there would be a fire.” How[Zhou] Xuandescribed dreams were all of this sort.
Out of ten, he would be right eight or nine times. His generation compared him to the divination of[Zhu] Jianping, and so his other deeds do not need to be mentioned.
During the waning years of theBrightEmperor, he died.
